---
title: "Evidence Register"
subtitle: "Processed evidence records from discovery interviews"
page-layout: full
---

::: {.page-context}
::: {.pc-crumb}
Evidence <span class="sep">›</span> Evidence Register
:::
::: {.pc-supports}
**This is the real evidence layer.** Claim strengths across the site are computed from the records
here.
:::
::: {.pc-row}
<span class="chip chip-neutral no-dot">Empty at v0.1 — awaiting fieldwork</span>
<span class="pc-next">[See how it rolls up](claims-confidence.qmd)</span>
:::
:::

Each record maps a discovery interview to a **claim** and a **decision**. To add evidence, run an
interview (`templates/discovery_interview_template.md`), log it in
`data/raw/discovery_interviews.csv`, then create records in `data/processed/discovery_evidence.csv`
using `templates/evidence_capture_template.md`. **Do not label a proposal assertion as validated
evidence.**

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| echo: false
import sys, pathlib
_root = pathlib.Path.cwd()
while _root != _root.parent and not (_root / "src").is_dir():
    _root = _root.parent
sys.path.insert(0, str(_root))
from IPython.display import HTML
from src.registers import load_discovery_evidence
from src.utils import load_raw

evidence = load_discovery_evidence()
interviews = load_raw("discovery_interviews.csv")

def metric(label, value, sub=""):
    sub_html = f'<div class="metric-sub">{sub}</div>' if sub else ""
    return (f'<div class="metric"><div class="metric-label">{label}</div>'
            f'<div class="metric-value">{value}</div>{sub_html}</div>')

HTML(
    '<div class="metric-row">'
    + metric("Discovery interviews", len(interviews), "logged")
    + metric("Evidence records", len(evidence), "mapped to claims")
    + metric("Claims with evidence", 0 if evidence.empty else evidence["claim_id"].nunique(),
             "supported or refuted")
    + "</div>"
)
```

## Discovery evidence records

```{python}
#| label: register-table
#| echo: false
if evidence.empty:
    HTML('<div class="callout callout-note"><div class="callout-body" '
         'style="padding:1rem 1.2rem">No evidence records yet. The pipeline is ready and validated; '
         'it is waiting for the first discovery interview. The table will populate here once '
         '<code>data/processed/discovery_evidence.csv</code> has rows.</div></div>')
else:
    display(evidence)
```

The expected columns are: `evidence_id`, `interview_id`, `decision_id`, `claim_id`, `source_type`,
`source_name`, `date_collected`, `quality_score`, `bias_risk`, `supports_or_refutes`,
`evidence_summary`, `confidence_impact`, `notes`.

## Legacy synthetic register (illustrative only)

The original scaffold shipped a small **synthetic** register to exercise the scoring machinery. It is
kept here for continuity and to demonstrate the roll-up computation, but it is **not real evidence**
and does not feed the decision pages.

```{python}
#| label: legacy
#| echo: false
from src.utils import load_sample
from src.evidence import summarise_evidence_by_claim

legacy = load_sample("evidence_register.csv")
summarise_evidence_by_claim(legacy)
```

::: {.callout-warning appearance="simple" icon=false}
**Two layers, kept distinct.** The **discovery evidence** above is the real operating layer (empty at
v0.1). The **legacy synthetic register** is illustrative scaffolding with older claim ids
(`H-CRM-AI`, …). Real claims live in `data/sample/claim_register.csv`.
:::
